text: the '3-6 months' rule is so arbitrary it hurts
it's not about the number, it's about not having to put a car repair on a credit card
lol true, but that's exactly why people get stuck. they see '3 months' and quit before they save a single grand.
wait, is it better to have the cash sitting in a high-yield savings account or just pay down debt?
depends on the interest rate on the debt.
if you're at 20% APR on a card, that's an emergency. pay that off first.
but if it's low interest student loans, i'd rather have the liquid cash for when life inevitably breaks something.
this. people always forget that liquidity has value.
what if i have no savings and a massive bill? asking for a friend lol
the classic mistake is putting it all in the market and then needing it when the market is down 20%.
everyone's a genius until the transmission blows in the same month as a medical bill.
